titleStmt.title: Letter from Alfred Bryne to Augustine Birrell, 30 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alfred Byrne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from Alfred Byrne (1882-1956), a member of parliament in Dublin harbor district and member of the Irish Parliamentary Party to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the Chief Secretary for Ireland. The letter recommends Charles P. O'Neill (b. 1867), an Irish solicitor, for the vacant position of Chief Crown Solicitor due to the death of former solicitor Malchy Kelly (1850-1916). The letter recommends O'Neill, noting the death of Kelly. It also notes that a word from Birrell will secure O'Neill the position and that his appointment would be received with great gratitude. It ends thanking Birrell on behalf of the Irish Parliamentary Party.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5729/8

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
60 North Strand Rd 30th March 1916 Dear Mr Birrell Friends innumerable of
Charles P O Neill Solicitor
(Chairman of Pembroke Council)
have called on me to write
to you on his behalf for
the Post of Crown Solicitor
now vacant — a word from
you they say would be
sufficient to gain him the
appointment and I believe
if appointed, it will be
the most popular appointment
ever made and will be
received with gratification
by his numerous friends
in the Irish Party With best wishes
yours sincerely Alfred Byrne
A Birrell Esq M P
